title: Sequential FDR (SFDR) posterior-error gate for Loop A promotion
source: LdP newer-corpus verification (4 parallel read-only agents over the added 2012-2026 papers + LdP REFERENCE CODE in D:/PROJECTS/QuantTradingDocs/code, 2026-06-03). Conversation: 'verifica la nueva informacion antes de implementar'.
why: The 2026 standard's 'which control to apply' table places SFDR (Sequential FDR) as the appropriate posterior-error control for INDUSTRIAL strategy selection (recruitment/defunding by an investment committee) — which is exactly Loop A (manufacture many candidates, deploy survivors, reversible defunding). FWER is for foundational discoveries; our case is SFDR. DSR is still preferred over generic FWER (it accounts for trial heterogeneity V[SR_k]). Also: the 2026 FDR paper proves search-and-selection makes in-sample FDR NON-IDENTIFIABLE unless the search intensity K is modeled (true FDR can exceed 80%) — hardening B0121's 'count N honestly' into a correctness condition.
value_rationale: Adds the standard-endorsed posterior-error control for our exact (industrial) setting, complementing DSR. PBO (B0122) becomes a complement, not the headline.
scope: Implement SFDR posterior-error control at the Loop A promotion gate (over the survivor config family)
Encode honest search intensity K (effective trials) — search-adjusted FDR is non-identifiable from the survivor cross-section alone
Keep DSR (B0132) as the per-run metric and B0121 DSR+MinTRL NO-GO; re-scope B0122 PBO as a complement
recommendation: Add SFDR alongside DSR at the promotion gate; pair with honest-K accounting. Complements (does not replace) B0121/B0122.
out_of_scope: Replacing DSR (DSR stays, preferred over generic FWER). The PSR/DSR formula.

external_refs.label: SFDR for industrial selection; FDR non-identifiability under search

history.reason: verification: SFDR is the 2026-standard posterior-error gate for Loop A (industrial selection); honest-K required
chore(backlog): provenance for B0139-B0145 verification commit
